mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment grows steadily over the prior six months, it signals a robust labor market that supports household income and corporate revenues, leading to systematically higher positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods without such payroll growth.
evidence_refs: fact:US payrolls have been rising into early 2014 during an ongoing expansion
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansion periods defined by six consecutive months of PAYEMS growth, the share of positive SPX 20-day returns does not exceed the unconditional 65.5% baseline by at least 5 percentage points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises by at least 50 basis points within three months from a level below 1%, it indicates the start of a tightening cycle that increases discount rates and pressures equity valuations, producing a higher frequency of negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns immediately after the tightening moves.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >= 0.5 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window from a starting level below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:As of early 2014 the funds rate is near zero and tightening discussions are active
failure_condition: Across all instances where FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.5 percentage points over 3 months from below 1%, the S&P 500 records positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month acceleration in real retail and food services sales signals strengthening final demand that boosts revenue expectations and risk appetite, resulting in above-baseline positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as equity markets quickly price the demand surprise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS grows at an annualized rate at least 2 percentage points faster over the last 3 months than over the prior 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales have been trending higher into early 2014 indicating firm consumer demand
failure_condition: During post-shock recovery periods identified by GDP rebounding from a recent trough, instances of RSAFS 3-month acceleration as defined do not show at least a 7 percentage point increase in the share of positive 5-day SPX returns relative to the unconditional distribution over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts for at least three consecutive months, it signals a broad-based slowdown in real activity and manufacturing profits that increases recession risk, causing S&P 500 60-day forward returns to be negative more often than in periods without such production declines.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO index level declines for at least 3 consecutive months on a month-over-month basis
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is modestly above 2017=100 in early 2014 after recovering from the 2008–09 recession
failure_condition: Across all identified episodes of three consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, the S&P 500 records positive 60-day forward returns in at least 50% of cases, and the average 60-day return during such episodes does not fall below the unconditional mean by at least 3 percentage points over any 10-year sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation pressures measured by CPI accelerate while the federal funds rate remains anchored near zero, real short-term rates fall and financial conditions ease, leading to systematically higher S&P 500 20-day forward returns as investors anticipate stronger nominal growth and continued policy support.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-on-year inflation rises by at least 0.5 percentage points over 6 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:As of early 2014 CPI is rising moderately while the policy rate is near zero
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL year-on-year inflation increases by at least 0.5 percentage points over 6 months with FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, the average S&P 500 20-day forward return does not exceed the unconditional 20-day average by at least 2 percentage points and the share of positive returns does not exceed 70% over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5% while industrial production is still growing on a year-on-year basis, it signals a tight but not yet contracting labor market consistent with solid demand and manageable wage pressure, producing predominantly positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns as the expansion persists.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 6.5% and INDPRO 12-month percent change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:By early 2014 unemployment is trending down near the Fed threshold while output continues to grow
failure_condition: During months when UNRATE is below 6.5% and INDPRO 12-month growth is positive, the share of positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns does not exceed the unconditional 65.5% baseline by at least 5 percentage points over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in housing starts from depressed levels signals renewed residential investment and construction employment, which amplifies aggregate demand and boosts equity risk appetite, generating above-average positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns when HOUST rebounds after a multi-quarter slump.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month moving average rises by at least 10% from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts in early 2014 are recovering from post-crisis lows but remain below prior-cycle peaks
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery periods where the HOUST 6-month average has risen by at least 10% from its 12-month low, the average S&P 500 20-day forward return does not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1.5 percentage points and the hit rate of positive 20-day returns does not exceed 70% over any 4-year sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When real activity and labor markets strengthen while the policy rate remains at the zero lower bound, market participants expect future tightening and begin to discount higher discount rates, so the combination of rising payrolls and ultra-low FEDFUNDS from already low unemployment leads to weaker S&P 500 5-day forward returns as equities temporarily digest policy risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS has grown for at least 6 consecutive months, UNRATE is below 7%, and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:Into early 2014 payrolls are expanding and unemployment falling while the funds rate remains near zero
regime_hint: transition from monetary easing to tightening
failure_condition: Across all days satisfying the trigger conditions, the S&P 500 records negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 45% of cases and the average 5-day return is not at least 0.3 percentage points lower than the unconditional mean over any 3-year rolling window.
